  • Custom extensions hosted on SAP BTP – applications built for example with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, deployed to the SAP BTP and served using the Approuter of SAP Build Work Zone. (For details on building such extensions, see Building Pro-Code Extensions for SAP Mobile Start)
  • SAP Fiori apps from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition running through an SAP Cloud Connector using a Principal Propagation Destination – these are tunneled through the BTP subaccount and are therefore covered by the same Application entry

The key distinction is that automatic coverage applies to applications that are part of – or tunneled through – the SAP BTP subaccount and its corresponding SAP Cloud Identity Services Application. Applications that run on their own infrastructure and have their own independent trust relationship with SAP Cloud Identity Services are not automatically covered, regardless of whether they are launched in embedded ("in-place") mode or opened directly ("ex-place"). A prominent example is SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: even when it is iFramed within the SAP Build Work Zone shell, it has its own Application entry in SAP Cloud Identity Services and must be explicitly added to the Native-Web Trust group.

For all such web applications – SAP solutions with their own IAS Application entry, third-party apps, or any app not part of the Work Zone BTP subaccount – administrators add them to the trust configuration in SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication as described in the setup steps below.

Administrator Setup

Step 1: Verify That IAS-Based Authentication Is Active

Before enabling the feature, confirm that SAP Build Work Zone is using SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication as its identity provider. If this is not yet the case, follow the migration steps referenced in the prerequisite section above.

Step 2: Enable the Feature Flag in SAP Mobile Services

In the SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit, navigate to the Client Settings. In the Feature Flags section, locate the entry named "MobiletoWebSingleSign-On" and activate it.

Screenshot showing the highlighted feature flag in the activated state in the Mobile Services Admin Cockpit.

For reference, the corresponding documentation can be found here: Single Sign-On for Web Apps – SAP Help

Step 3: Add Trusted Web Applications in SAP Cloud Identity Services

For web applications that are not automatically covered through the SAP Build Work Zone application (see the previous section), you can add them to the trust configuration:

  1. Sign in to the administration console for SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  2. Go to Applications & Resources → Applications.
  3. Select the application entry that corresponds to your SAP Build Work Zone instance.
  4. Go to the Trust tab, then navigate to Application APIs → Dependencies → Native-Web Trust.
  5. Click Add and select the application with which you want to establish trust.
  6. Save your selection.

Screenshot of SAP Cloud Identity Services to maintain Native-Web Trust Settings

Users opening applications from this trust list in the in-app browser will now benefit from the seamless Mobile-to-Web SSO flow.

Step 4: Verify the Redirect URIs of Trusted Applications

As described in the prerequisite section, SAP Cloud Identity Services validates the URL of the target web application against the allowed redirect URIs of the trusted application entry. For SAP-delivered solutions – such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – the redirect URIs are typically pre-configured correctly by SAP and no further action is needed.

However, for custom-built applications or third-party web apps that rely on the general SAP Business Technology Platform application entry in SAP Cloud Identity Services, you should verify that the application's URL is covered by the configured redirect URI patterns. If the URL does not match any permitted pattern, the Mobile-to-Web SSO flow will not complete successfully for that application.

You can review and adjust the redirect URIs in the SAP Cloud Identity Services administration console under the application's configuration. For the rules and wildcard syntax supported for redirect URIs, refer to the SAP Help documentation: Redirect URIs and Post Logout Redirect URI Rules (SAP Help).

Summary

With the Mobile-to-Web SSO feature, Joule Work mobile app version 3.0 closes the session gap between the native app context and the in-app browser – cleanly, securely, and with full administrator control.

Key takeaways:

  • The feature eliminates the need for long-lived "Remember me" cookies on the identity provider, which are a security concern and not universally available.
  • It is based on short-lived, one-time use SSO tokens issued and validated by SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication, which acts as the common trust anchor.
  • The feature is optional and in control by the customer: an administrator (de-/) activates it via a feature flag in SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit of Joule Work mobile app and controls which web applications participate via the Native-Web Trust configuration in SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication.
  • SAP Build Work Zone web UIs and tunneled embedded business apps are automatically covered. Additional apps can be added with a few clicks.
  • The feature is compatible with all in-app browser variants in Joule Work mobile app and all trusted web applications, regardless of whether they are SAP solutions or custom apps.

If you need further assistance configuring and making use of this new feature in the Joule Work mobile app, feel free to contact our product management team using jouleworkmobile@sap.com, which will be able to suggest the next steps for your scenario.

Further Reading

  • Joule Work mobile app – Single Sign-On for Web Apps (SAP Help)
  • Switching to SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication (SAP Help)
  • Demystifying the Common Super Domain for SAP Mobile Start (SAP Community Blog)
  • Building Pro-Code Extensions for SAP Mobile Start – Leveraging Mobile Native Features (SAP Community Blog)
  • SAP Mobile Experience Community Page

entry.title: Optimizing SAP Fiori Elements for Mobile Phone Users
entry.content:
Published: 09th June 2026 | Reading time: 5 minutes 

In today's digital era, let me ask you a simple question: Which device do you choose while traveling or enjoying coffee in a breakout area—your laptop or your mobile phone? 

Or imagine you're a quality inspector in a manufacturing plant, constantly moving between machines throughout the day. Would you carry a bulky laptop, or would you prefer the convenience of a mobile phone? 

I'm willing to bet you chose mobile. And you're not alone. Mobile phones have become our go-to productivity tools—handy, accessible, and ready wherever we are, whether on a bus, train, or at a coffee table. 

The Mobile Gap We Need to Close 

As mobile devices dominate our work lives, our customers and users naturally expect a seamless experience when using SAP Fiori elements applications. 

Current State: Good, But Not Great 

Today's SAP Fiori elements applications are responsive—they adjust controls based on screen size and device type. That's a great foundation, but it's not the complete picture. 

The Reality Check 

Despite the responsive design, users still face friction: 

  • Multi-dimensional scrolling: Navigating horizontally and vertically between filter fields 
  • Table challenges: Squinting and swiping through rows and columns 
  • Compromised UX: Functional, yes. Delightful? Not quite. 

This gap between "it works" and "it works beautifully" became the catalyst for our innovation. 

SAP Fiori Elements Mobile Phone Optimization Goes Beyond Responsive Design 

Introducing our game-changer: SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization—a product designed to deliver the best-in-class mobile experience your users deserve. 

List report page using the SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization  

 

Object page using the SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization  

What Makes It Special 

This isn't just about shrinking desktop interfaces. It's about reimagining the entire mobile experience: 

  • Native mobile feel: Controls optimized for touch interactions 
  • Intuitive navigation: No more frustrating scrolling marathons 
  • Smart layouts: Content adapts intelligently to mobile screens 
  • Performance first: Fast, responsive, and fluid 

Addressing Your Concerns 

You might be thinking: "Sounds good, but what about the development effort and learning curve?" 

Good news – we've designed this with you in mind: 

  • Zero additional coding: Works out of the box 
  • Automatic optimization: Smart defaults that just work 
  • Familiar patterns: Built on SAP Fiori elements concepts you already know 

Why Mobile Optimization Matters 

Mobile optimization isn't just a feature—it's a necessity in today's mobile-first world. With SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization we're not just meeting expectations, we're setting a new standard for mobile enterprise applications. 

What's Next 

Stay tuned for our upcoming series where we'll dive deep into key features and capabilities. 

Join the Early Customer Care (EAC) Program 

Ready to experience the difference and help share the product? 

Sign up and share your feedback: SAP Customer Influence

Have questions or thoughts? Drop a comment below. 

Siva Rama Krishna Pendyala is a Product Manager on the SAP UX Foundation team. Connect on LinkedIn.

entry.title: Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1 is out
entry.content:
With the latest release of Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1, the app provides exciting features for both, the New Experience (Engagement Layer), as well as the SAP Build Work Zone Experience – formally called SAP Mobile Start.

Let me start with the features that are valid for both experiences.
Based on customer feedback, the app allows you now to customise the Support Section on the Settings screen. This allows you to add additional information for your users, in case they have any support needs. Administrators can easily add information, such as phone numbers, email addresses, and websites for further end user support. Once configured, the info is displayed in the Profile and Settings screen as well as during the onboarding process. The feature can be added and configured via SAP Mobile Services or via Mobile Device Management (MDM). Please also check our SAP Help page for details.

We also have updated the supported languages for SAP Joule Work and added: Greek, Croatian, Indonesian, Romanian, Ukrainian, and Vietnamese.

Now, let us have a look at the features that are relevant for the SAP Build Work Zone Experience.
First and most important, you can now directly access workspaces from SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - via a dedicated screen that is available in the navigation bar. This ensures that you get direct access to your most needed content from anywhere in the app. In case you work with many workspaces, you can easily filter them, for instance if you only want to see your favourites – or search for them. As soon as this feature got enabled by the admin,  the SAP Build Work Zone Workspaces screen will be visible.  

Screenshot: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition – Workspaces

Second, users can now change the language of external SAP Build Work Zone tiles, like news and to-dos directly inside the app, from the settings screen.

Now, let us have a look at the new features for our Joule Work New Experience.
As introduced in my last blog “Joule Work, mobile app is here”, Spaces are one of the key elements of the New Experience. Lets recap: Spaces are dynamic, intent-driven workspaces for more complex business problems. They are composed in real time to bring the right data from different systems, tools, and context together for a specific goal, freeing people from working in multiple applications and passive UIs. With the new release, you can now pin your favourite spaces to the top of the spaces list, what will make it much easier for you to access your favourite content. To do so, just do a long tap on the space that you want to pin and the menu appears. Here, you can easily rename your space, delete it or pin/ unpin it. Joule Work New Experience is currently available within an Early Adapter Care program.

 Screenshot: Pin favourite Spaces to the top of the list

I hope you will enjoy these features in the Joule Work mobile app.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager: New Release Strategy Incoming
entry.content:
If you're working with SAP Service and Asset Manager (SSAM) — whether as a customer, partner, or someone evaluating the solution — there is news worth paying attention to. We are changing the way we release SSAM, and I believe this change will make a real, tangible difference to everyone who uses it.

Let me walk you through what's changing and why it matters.

 

 

From Two Major Releases a Year to One — With a Purpose

Until now, SAP Service and Asset Manager shipped on a cadence of two major releases per year. We are moving to a new model:

 

1 Major release + 3 Minor releases per year

This is not a slowdown. It is a deliberate shift in how we think about delivering value. Here is what the new model means in practice:

- Minor releases carry fewer new features — they focus on fixes, refinements, and incremental improvements

- No MDK version update in minor releases (security patches excepted) — the underlying Mobile Development Kit stays stable between major releases

- Mobile backend updates in major and 2nd minor release – Providing two crisp client-only upgrades

 For customers and partners, this means a much more predictable upgrade experience. Minor releases are safe and lightweight. Major releases are the moments where new capabilities and platform upgrades arrive.

 

The Release Roadmap

Here is how the new cadence looks across the next release cycles:

 

 

 

 

 

MDK Stabilization: One Upgrade Per Year

The Mobile Development Kit (MDK) is the technical foundation that SSAM is built on. Under the new strategy, MDK version upgrades are aligned exclusively with major releases — meaning one upgrade per year at most.

For anyone who has managed SSAM deployments, this matters. It means your environment stays on a known, stable MDK version between major cycles. No unexpected changes mid-year. No cascading compatibility issues you didn't plan for.

 

What You Should Do Now

If you are on an earlier SSAM version or planning a new deployment, the roadmap above gives you a clear view of what is coming and when — use it to align your project timeline with the right release.

I am excited about where SAP Service and Asset Manager is going. A more frequent release rhythm to bring exciting improvements, and major milestones on the horizon. This is a product being built to last.

 

Questions or thoughts? I would love to hear from the community — drop a comment below.
